titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Jonathan Galassi Papers
titlestmt.author: by Rosemary K. J. Davis, Jim Fisher, and Greyson Gates

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Thursday November 5, 2020 at 04:18

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

did.abstract: The Jonathan Galassi Papers, which span from 1958-2016, contain writings, including notebooks, drafts, translations, research materials, and correspondence The papers also contain photographs, printed materials, audiovisual materials, electronic media, and other papers by or relating to Jonathan Galassi.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The materials are open for research.
Box 2 (videocassette): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 3 (born digital): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Chris Calhoun Agency on the Edwin J. Beinecke Book Fund, 2018.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two series: I. Correspondence and Personal Papers, 1958-2015. II. Writings and Translations, 1892-2016.

bioghist.head: Jonathan Galassi
bioghist.p: Jonathan Galassi (1949-) is an American poet and publisher. Born in Seattle and raised in Massachusetts, Galassi graduated from Harvard where he served as editor of the Harvard Lampoon . After working at publishing houses including Random House and Houghton Mifflin, Galassi now serves as president at Farrar, Straus & Giroux, where he started working in 1986. Galassi's poetry and translations have been widely published and 1989, he was awarded a Guggeneheim Fellowship.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Jonathan Galassi Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Jonathan Galassi Papers, which span from 1958-2016, contain writings, including notebooks, drafts, translations, research materials, and correspondence The papers also contain photographs, printed materials, audiovisual materials, electronic media, and other papers by or relating to Jonathan Galassi.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Jonathan Galassi Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material within this series maintains the original organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence, writings, clippings, and personal items related to Jonathan Galassi's life and work.

did.unittitle: Typescripts of school essays and autograph notes
Pocket diary, address books, and wallet with pictures, IDs, and correspondence
Address books and travel wallet with passports for Galassi and Susan Galassi
The Exonian student newspaper from Phillips Exeter Academy and The New York Post featuring September 11 attack
The New York Times newspaper featuring September 11 attacks

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Box 2 (videocassette): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.
Box 3 (computer disks): Restricted fragile material. Access copies of digital files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Use of originals is restricted. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

did.unittitle: VHS tape- Jason Shinder and Jonathan Galassi, Attitude Art

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Restricted Fragile Material. Reference copies of electronic files may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes schoolwork, scholarship applications, report cards, and copy of birth certificate.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Poems include "After the Wedding, A Toast," and "Hero in Winter."

did.unittitle: Summer of '90 mostly holograph poem manuscripts with NYU exam booklet

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains poem manuscripts of Shirt of Fat, Syllogism, Sleepless Nights, Friend and Lover, Backlighting, Bald Peak, Where the Pavement Ends, Face, Wind, Finer Sail, Your Voice, Carousel, Jerusalem, Villa Sciarra, and Susan.

did.unittitle: Notebook from Christ's College Cambridge, England with poem drafts and journal entries

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes loose pages of holograph and typescript poetry and prose with related correspondence.

did.unittitle: News articles and reviews written by Galassi
J.W. Galassi notebook of early poetry, school essays, and prose

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of Four Ape, Revere Beach, Driving in Duxbury, The Fishtank, Waking Sometimes at 5:30, The Bulls, In Memory of W. Ernest Gillespie, Antony and Cleopatra, For Catherine Burke, Horace I, On Perugino's Saint Mary Magdalene dei Pazzi, First Snow, Tennis, Villanellette: A Torture, Pictures 1964-1965, Waves, Class Ode, and To One Recuperating from a Nervous Breakdown.

did.unittitle: New York Review featuring Galassi and poem manuscripts

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of Franco Fortini's "Sansepolcro" translated by Galassi and "Greetings, Friends!" edited poem manuscript.

did.unittitle: Family photo album and other materials

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Material within this series maintains the original organization found at time of acquisition.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains drafts, galley proofs, and typescripts of Jonathan Galassi's writings along with related correspondence and reviews.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of Times at Bellosguardo; review of A Violent Life by Pier Paolo Pasolini; A Hymn of Non-Attainment; Notes on Reading; Elizabeth Bishop: A Student's Recollections; Our Wives; The Double Agent; Springtime in Paris; French Publishing; Determined Forms; Self-Involvement.

did.unittitle: Early published reviews and edited manuscripts
Translation manuscripts of Sergio Salvi's poems with correspondence and originals in Italian

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of Yachting, Coccus, Ocular Intercourse, The King of Wedders, and Young Mortality.

did.unittitle: Roger Klein Award correspondence including nominations, coverage, and reception bulletin
"The Second Life of Art" manuscript pages, royalty statements, grant applications, and related correspondence
POETRY magazine translation manuscripts with royalty statements with correspondence
Translation manuscripts of Salvatore Quasimodo's poems with holograph drafts, correspondence

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of And Suddenly It's Evening; Another Green River; Autumn; Horses of Moon and Volcanoes; Hungrily; Imitation of Joy; Island; Maybe the Heart; Mirror; Often a Coastline; O My Gentle Animals; Tree; This Pilgrim; In the Old Light of the Tides; The Grass Rests; Old Winter; Earth; For My Human Smell; Onset of Puberty; To My Land; Shepherd of Air; and Sorrow of Things I Don't Know.

did.unittitle: "Otherwise" correspondence, royalty statements, reviews, and manuscript pages

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes royalty reports, event brochures, reviews, and contract.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains materials relating to introduction of translation of Montale's Altri Versi; poem drafts of Elegy, Montale at War, Piano Sonatina, Gulls, In the Void, For Mme. Gerti T.F. 1st Fragment, and Silent Music.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of Music; You Were No Spring River; The Trees of Our Childhood; Leopardi, L'INFINITO; Not the Poem for the Mind; A Leaf; Song; We Won't Die Out; Founder; Forecast; Memory and Dream; Success; The Art of Life; The Knight Speaks to His Lady Susan; and Driving in Duxbury.

scopecontent.p: Contains manuscripts of Summer, The Customs, New Stanzas, Times at Bellosguardo, News from Mount Amiata, The Storm, The Earrings, The Fringe of Hair, Personae Separatae, The Orchard, Coast of Versilia, The Hitler Spring, The Magnolia's Shadow, The Woodcock, The Eel, Anniversary, The Prisoner's Dream, On the Threshold, Cuttlefish Bones, to K., Portovenere, A Poem by Eugenio Montale, Eclogue, Arsenio, House by the Sea, The Dead, and Delta.
